name: show_whitespace
description: Display file content with visible whitespace characters for debugging indentation issues. Shows tabs, trailing spaces, and line endings. Supports actions: all (tabs + trailing spaces + line endings), tabs (show tabs as →), trailing (show trailing spaces), endings (show line endings), lines (show with line numbers only, like nl -ba). Supports --head and --tail for output limiting.
when_to_use: Use when you need to debug indentation issues (mixing tabs and spaces, wrong tab depth), verify whitespace after edits, or check for trailing whitespace. Replaces the cat -A / cat -e -t -v pattern with readable output showing tabs as → and trailing spaces as ·. The 'lines' action replaces nl -ba or cat -n for reading files with line numbers. The 'tabs' action is especially useful when troubleshooting indentation problems after inserting code, helping you quickly see if tab depth is correct.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Path to the file to inspect.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: 'all' (show tabs, trailing spaces, line endings, default), 'tabs' (show tabs as →), 'trailing' (show trailing spaces), 'endings' (show line endings as $), 'lines' (show with line numbers only like nl -ba).

parameters.name: --head
parameters.description: Show only the first N lines.

parameters.name: --tail
parameters.description: Show only the last N lines.

examples.expected: Shows the file with tabs as →, trailing spaces as ·, and line numbers. Useful to verify correct tab/space usage after inserting code.
Shows only tabs (as →) with line numbers. Use to quickly verify tab depth (indentation level) after inserting new code blocks.
Shows tabs in the first 30 lines only. Useful when debugging indentation in a specific section of a large file.
Shows file with line numbers (like nl -ba). Replaces 'nl -ba file' or 'cat -n'.
Shows the last 20 lines with line numbers.
Shows trailing whitespace as ·. Use to clean up files before committing.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces the cat -A / cat -e -t -v debugging pattern (which outputs hard-to-read special characters) with readable visual markers. The 'tabs' action directly addresses the common indentation-debugging loop: insert code → verify indentation with cat -A → fix with sed → verify again (3-5+ steps). With show_whitespace tabs, you can verify indentation in one step with clear → markers. The 'lines' action replaces nl -ba / cat -n patterns (saving a step). Output limiting prevents token overflow from large files. Combined with file_patch for correct indentation from the start, this eliminates the entire indentation-fixing workflow.
